15 May 2026 Military News

Operations

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Georgia National Guard Counterdrug Task Force assists investigation that seized 13-plus kilograms of illegal drugs Army News 15 May 2026 -- Members of the Georgia National Guard's Counterdrug Task Force assisted the Georgia Bureau of Investigation and other state and local law enforcement agencies in an investigation resulting in the seizure of more than 13 kilograms of fentanyl, cocaine and other drugs, along with 10 firearms, cash and other property.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • US Navy Warns Epic Fury Burning Through Money and Manpower - Reports Sputnik 15 May 2026 -- The US Navy may have to cut training, routine operations and personnel by July unless Congress sends more money for Operation Epic Fury, American media reported.

  • Trump Says He Would Accept Iran Suspending Enrichment For 20 Years RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- President Donald Trump has signaled he would be agreeable to Iran suspending its nuclear program for 20 years.
  • Trump Suggests Losing Patience With Tehran, But Appears To Ease Demands On Uranium RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump expressed frustration with Tehran for its refusal to accept a US peace deal, but he appeared to back off demands that Iran must allow its supply of enriched uranium to be removed from the country.

  • Iranian FM says situation around Strait of Hormuz "very complicated" People's Daily 15 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi said here on Friday that the situation around the Strait of Hormuz was "very complicated," local media reported.
  • Iran FM: Transfer of enriched uranium not on agenda, US cannot be trusted Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran and the United States have almost reached an impasse over the "very complicated" issue of uranium enrichment, which is not on the agenda of talks for the time being.
  • Iran slams neighbors' betrayal during US-Israeli aggression Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Iran has censured certain neighboring countries for their betrayal during the recent illegal US-Israeli war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iranian Foreign Minister Says US Seeking Continued Talks RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi on May 15 said Tehran had received messages from the United States indicating that the Trump administration is seeking continued talks aimed at ending the Middle East war.
  • Former IRGC Commander Jafari Lays Out Iran's Peace Conditions RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- Mohammad Ali Jafari, the former commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC), has said there will be no further peace negotiations "until the war is over on all fronts, sanctions are lifted, frozen funds are released, damages caused by the war are compensated, and Iran's sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz is recognized."
  • Iran's Araghchi meets PM Modi, holds regional security talks on BRICS sidelines Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has met with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi, discussing regional stability amid a series of high-level diplomatic engagements on the sidelines of the BRICS foreign ministers' meeting.

  • China Says Content, Timing 'Not Right' On US-Backed UN Hormuz Resolution RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- China's ambassador to the UN on May 15 criticized a proposed US-Bahraini resolution on the ⁠Strait of Hormuz, ⁠that would have demanded that Iran cease attacks and mining in the crucial waterway.
  • China's top diplomat says Strait of Hormuz should be reopened as soon as possible Global Times 15 May 2026 -- China maintains that on the basis of sustaining the ceasefire, the Strait of Hormuz should be reopened as soon as possible, and at the same time believes that the fundamental solution to issues concerning the strait lies in the realization of a permanent and comprehensive ceasefire, said China's top diplomat Wang Yi on Friday.
  • FM responds to whether China agrees to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz Global Times 15 May 2026 -- When responding to a foreign media reporter's question stating that the White House claims that during the China-US leaders' meeting, China agreed to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and whether the Chinese Foreign Ministry could confirm that information, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday that China's position on the Iran situation is very clear. The conflict has inflicted severe losses on the people in Iran and other regional countries. With the spillover still expanding, the conflict has put a heavy strain on global economic growth, supply chains, international trade order and the stability of global energy supply, which hurts the common interests of the international community.
  • War on Iran should not have started, no point in continuing it: China Press TV 15 May 2026 -- China says it sees "no point" for the United States to continue its war against Iran, as the conflict is taking a heavy toll on global stability and energy security.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on the Iran Situation PRC MofA 15 May 2026
  • Chinese FM responds to whether China, US leaders talk about Iran during exchanges on views on major international and regional issues, including Middle East situation Global Times 15 May 2026 -- Q: It is reported that during President Trump's visit to China, the two presidents exchanged views on major international and regional issues, including the situation in the Middle East. Did the two sides talk about Iran? What is China's view on the current Iran situation?

  • 26 world leaders issue statement on normalizing Strait of Hormuz KOREA.net 15 May 2026 -- Twenty-six countries including Korea have issued a joint statement urging normalized operations in the Strait of Hormuz, buoyed by U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping agreeing on the need to reopen the strait.
  • UAE failed to push GCC states to join a coordinated war against Iran: Report Press TV 15 May 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates has failed in an attempt to convince other Arab member states of the Persian Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) to join Abu Dhabi in a coordinated military campaign against the Islamic Republic.
  • UAE Rejects Tehran's 'Attempts To Justify' Its 'Terrorist Attacks' RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates (UAE) rejected what it called Tehran's "attempts to justify" its attacks on Gulf countries after Iran accused Abu Dhabi of being an "active partner" in the US military campaign.
  • The crisis in the Strait of Hormuz has triggered challenges across the world: UK statement at the UN Economic and Social Council UK FCDO 15 May 2026 -- Statement by Helen King, UK Ambassador to ECOSOC, at the UN Economic and Social Council meeting on Safeguarding energy and supply flows: Supporting global development through international cooperation.

  • Iran Is Smuggling Millions Of Liters Of Fuel To Pakistan Every Day RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- Iran is smuggling at least 6 million liters of fuel each day to neighboring Pakistan, traders and transporters in the South Asian country told RFE/RL, in violation of US sanctions on the Islamic republic.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Department of War Invests $191M to Expand and Enhance the Solid Rocket Motor Industrial Base DoD 15 May 2026 -- The DoW announced today the latest in a series of investments in the solid rocket motor industrial base: an April 20, 2026, investment of $27.3 million in DPA Title III funds to Pacific Scientific Energetic Materials Company (PacSci EMC), in Chandler, Arizona. It supports DoW's objectives to expand the munitions industrial base, bolster supply chain resiliency, and increase domestic production in strategic priority areas.
  • Navy Announces Commissioning of the Future USS Cleveland DoD 15 May 2026 -- The Navy will commission its newest Freedom-variant littoral combat ship (LCS), the future USS Cleveland (LCS 31), during a 10 a.m. EDT ceremony Saturday, May 16, 2026, in Cleveland, Ohio.
  • Challenge Dropped to Air Force's Fighter Jet Training Operations in Eastern Oregon US Dept. of Justice 15 May 2026 -- Earlier this month, the Oregon Natural Desert Association voluntarily dismissed its suit that alleged that the Air Force was illegally discharging countermeasures — chaff and flares — into waterways as part of its fighter jet training operations over the Paradise North area of eastern Oregon.
  • US Army Cancels 4,000-Troop Poland Deployment Amid Budget Hole Sputnik 15 May 2026 -- The US Army abruptly scrapped the planned deployment of more than 4,000 soldiers from the 2nd Armored Brigade Combat Team, 1st Cavalry Division, to Poland, American media reported.
  • U.S. Coast Guard's newest polar icebreaker returns to Seattle following 36-day deployment to Bering Sea US Coast Guard 15 May 2026 -- The crew of the U.S. Coast Guard's newest polar icebreaker, the USCGC Storis (WAGB 21), returned to their temporary Seattle home port Monday after a 36-day deployment to the Bering Sea.
  • Brazilian and U.S. Navies Conduct Bilateral Maritime Engagement in Atlantic Ocean US Navy 15 May 2026 -- The Brazilian and U.S. navies conducted a bilateral maritime engagement as part of U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command (USNAVSOUTH)/U.S. 4th Fleet's Southern Seas 2026 deployment in the Atlantic Ocean, May 12-13.
  • Africom Commander Says Continent at 'Crossroads' of Global Commerce, Security AFRICOM 15 May 2026 -- Air Force Gen. Dagvin R.M. Anderson, commander of U.S. Africa Command, updated Congress on the security environment of the world's second largest continent during a hearing before the Senate Armed Services Committee in Washington today.
  • NY Guard trains with Swedish partners during Northern Lights 26 Army News 15 May 2026 -- Eighty New York Army National Guard Soldiers spent their annual training working alongside Swedish soldiers in Sweden's sprawling forests.

  • NATO Assistant Secretary General for Defence Industry, Innovation and Armaments visits the Republic of Korea and Japan NATO 15 May 2026 -- Assistant Secretary General for Defence Industry, Innovation and Armaments, Tarja Jaakkola, visited the Republic of Korea and Japan (11-15 May 2026) for discussions to strengthen NATO's defence industrial cooperation.
  • TAF 66th Mechanised Brigade Showcases Readiness at NATO - Serbia Exercise 2026 NATO Allied Joint Force Command (JFC) Naples 15 May 2026 -- 15 May 2026. A contingent from the Turkish Armed Forces (TAF) 66th Mechanised Infantry Brigade has commenced activities at the Borovac Training Area and Base South as part of NATO-Serbia Exercise 2026 (NSE26). The 36-personnel unit, centred around an 18-man Motorised Infantry Platoon, is demonstrating high readiness for the modern battlefield.

  • Deepening cooperation, interoperability and continued support for Ukraine: Taras Kachka holds meeting with NATO delegation Ukraine Government 15 May 2026 -- Deputy Prime Minister for European and Euro-Atlantic Integration of Ukraine Taras Kachka met with a delegation from the NATO International Staff led by Director for Defence and Security Cooperation Piers Cazalet.
  • Olexandr Mischenko Held a Meeting with NATO Senior Representative in Ukraine Patrick Turner Ukraine Government 15 May 2026 -- On May 15, 2026, Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine Olexandr Mischenko held a meeting with NATO Senior Representative in Ukraine Patrick Turner.
  • Yulia Svyrydenko: Government scales up use of DOT-Chain Defence system for defense procurement Ukraine Government 15 May 2026 -- The Government has extended the pilot project for defense procurement via the DOT-Chain Defence system until October 24, 2027. This was announced by Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko.
  • Olexandr Mischenko Held a Meeting with NATO Senior Representative in Ukraine Patrick Turner Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 15 May 2026
  • Minister of National Defence, at the "Strategic Dialogue Romania - Poland - Ukraine: strengthening the Eastern Flank - Industry, Deterrence, and Reconstruction" Round Table Romania - Ministry of National Defence 15 May 2026 -- On Thursday, May 14th, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, participated in the "Strategic Dialogue Romania - Poland - Ukraine: strengthening the Eastern Flank - Industry, Deterrence, and Reconstruction" Round Table organized in the context of the international exhibition Black Sea Defense, Aerospace and Security (BSDA) 2026.

  • Taiwan launches final 600-tonne Anping-class patrol vessel CNA 15 May 2026 -- Taiwan on Friday launched the 12th and final 600-tonne Anping-class patrol vessel built for the Coast Guard Administration (CGA), marking the completion of the fleet under the government's indigenous shipbuilding program.
  • DPP, KMT spar over funding options for weapons excluded from budget bill CNA 15 May 2026 -- The ruling Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) and the main opposition Kuomintang (KMT) clashed Friday over how to fund weapons systems recently excluded from a supplementary budget bill, after the Cabinet said it was considering three options to pay for the equipment.

  • Investing in Australian innovation with undersea navigation technology Australia DOD 15 May 2026 -- The Australian Government is strengthening our nation's ability to operate autonomous underwater vehicles with three Australian companies awarded contracts to develop new undersea navigation technology.
  • US Marines begin Darwin rotation Australia DOD 15 May 2026 -- About 2000 United States Marine Corps personnel and sailors are set to call northern Australia home for the next six months after the 15th iteration of the Marine Rotational Force - Darwin (MRF-D) touched down in Australia's Top End.
  • Preparing Australian industry to support the Virginia class submarine combat system Australia DOD 15 May 2026 -- AUKUS in Australia is taking another significant step forward with the Albanese Government selecting Lockheed Martin Australia as the preferred Combat System Integration Partner for Australia's future fleet of sovereign Virginia class submarines.
  • Deep Maintenance and Modification Facility strengthens sovereign Defence capability Australia DOD 15 May 2026 -- In a major milestone for Australia's sovereign defence sustainment capability, the Albanese and Malinauskas Governments have officially opened the $200 million Deep Maintenance and Modification Facility at Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) Base Edinburgh, South Australia.

  • First Kiwi Space Activators announced New Zealand Government 15 May 2026 -- Space Minister Chris Penk today announced the first recipients of the Kiwi Space Activator, a pilot programme helping to move novel space technology from the lab to the skies.

  • Tsahkna meeting with US Under Secretary DiNanno: European Allies must assume greater responsibility for the continent's security Estonia MFA 15 May 2026 -- Today, 15 May, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna met in Estonia with US Under Secretary of State for Arms Control and International Security Thomas G. DiNanno.

  • Minister of National Defence received Ambassador of the United States to Lithuania HE Kara C. McDonald: the United States are walking away from the commitments to the Lithuanian security Lithuania MND 15 May 2026 -- Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas met with the Ambassador of the United States to Lithuania, HE Kara C. McDonald.
  • The President: We likely have the best-protected external border in the European Union President of the Republic of Lithuania 15 May 2026 -- On Friday, President Gitanas Nausėda, together with Finnish President Alexander Stubb, who is on a state visit to Lithuania, visited the Padvarionys Border Guard Station and the Border Guard School.
  • The President at the Lithuania-Finland Business Forum: Let us build the defense industry together President of the Republic of Lithuania 15 May 2026 -- On Friday in Vilnius, President Gitanas Nausėda, together with President Alexander Stubb of Finland, opened the Lithuania-Finland Business Forum dedicated to strengthening cooperation in the fields of the defense industry, security, and resilience.

  • Defence Minister's agenda during BSDA Romania - Ministry of National Defence 15 May 2026 -- On Thursday, May 14th, the Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a series of meetings with representatives of the defence industry and participated in several conferences and round tables organized in the context of Black Sea Defense, Aerospace and Security (BSDA) 2026 international exhibition.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Foreign Minister Sobers of Trinidad and Tobago US Dept. of State 15 May 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 15 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 15 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • 'Ludicrous': Israel seeks to sue New York Times over article on rape of Palestinian prisoners Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Israel's prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu and foreign minister Gideon Sa'ar have threatened to sue the New York Times over the publication of an article detailing rape and sexual torture of Palestinian women, men and children in Israeli detention.

  • Israeli minister announces illegal settlement plans in Lebanon, mass displacement of Palestinians Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Israeli far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir has announced that the Tel Aviv regime intends to establish illegal settlements in southern Lebanon while encouraging the mass displacement of Palestinians from the besieged Gaza Strip and the occupied West Bank.

  • Israel, Lebanon to Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days - US State Dept. Sputnik 15 May 2026 -- The ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon will be extended by 45 days to enable further progress in the negotiations, US State Department Spokesperson Thomas Pigott said on Friday.

  • Hamas, Islamic Jihad reaffirm adherence to resistance against Israel on Nakba anniversary Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Palestinian resistance movements Hamas and Islamic Jihad have reaffirmed their adherence to resistance against Israel until Palestine is liberated, urging the international community to stop the regime's "terrorism' which poses a "real threat" to the security and stability of the region and the world.

  • Israeli military strikes add to civilian suffering in Gaza, West Bank and Lebanon UN News 15 May 2026 -- Civilians across Gaza, the West Bank and Lebanon continue to face mounting hardship as conflict, displacement and ongoing military operations deepen humanitarian needs and strain already limited aid efforts, according to the latest updates from UN agencies.

  • Meetings Between the Governments of the United States, Lebanon, and Israel US Dept. of State 15 May 2026 -- On May 14 and 15, the United States facilitated two days of constructive talks between the State of Israel and the Republic of Lebanon at the Department of State.

  • The OIC General Secretariat Condemns the Storming of Al-Aqsa Mosque by Extremist Israeli Minister Ben-Gvir Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 15 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) condemned, in the strongest terms, the storming of the courtyards of the blessed Al-Aqsa Mosque by the extremist Itamar Ben-Gvir, a minister in the Israeli occupation government, at the head of groups of extremist settlers, raising the Israeli occupation flag and performing provocative rituals inside its courtyards, under the protection of the Israeli occupation forces, considering this a blatant aggression and deliberate provocation of the feelings of Muslims all over the world.
  • The OIC Reaffirms the Inalienable Rights of the Palestinian People on the 78th Anniversary of the Nakba Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 15 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) commemorates, on May 15 of each year, the Nakba that befell the Palestinian people in 1948 as a result of the declaration of the establishment of Israel, the occupying power, and the crimes of organized terrorism, genocide, ethnic cleansing, forced displacement, and the deliberate destruction of hundreds of Palestinian villages and cities, as well as the confiscation of the lands and properties of the indigenous Palestinian people.
  • Saudi Arabia Condemns Repeated Provocative Practices by Israeli Occupation Officials at Al-Aqsa Mosque SPA 15 May 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's condemnation of the repeated provocative practices carried out by officials of the Israeli occupation authorities against Al-Aqsa Mosque, most recently the storming of Al-Aqsa Mosque by an official from the Israeli occupation authorities under the protection of occupation police, as well as another official raising the flag of the occupation authorities within the courtyards of Al-Aqsa Mosque
  • General Secretariat statement on 78th anniversary of Palestinian Nakba League of Arab States 15 May 2026 -- The 78th anniversary of the Palestinian Nakba of 1948 falls on 15 May, marking the day that witnessed the establishment of Israel, the occupying Power, which perpetrated a fully fledged crime of ethnic cleansing resulting in the martyrdom of more than 15,000 Palestinians, the destruction of 531 Palestinian towns and villages, and the displacement and forced uprooting of more than 957,000 Palestinians from their cities and villages.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Syria, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Cuba, Somalia UNOCHA 15 May 2026 -- At Security Council, UN relief chief calls Syria 'a country on the precipice of promise'

  • Afghan mothers and children face worsening hunger crisis, WFP warns UN News 15 May 2026 -- Afghanistan's deepening malnutrition crisis is pushing mothers and children to the brink, the UN World Food Programme has warned, as mass returns from neighbouring countries and severe funding shortfalls overwhelm already strained humanitarian operations.

  • The OIC General Secretariat Welcomes the Agreement on the Exchange of Detainees in Yemen Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 15 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) welcomed the agreement on the exchange of detainees in Yemen, which was signed on Thursday, 14 May 2026, in the Jordanian capital, Amman.
  • HE GCCSG Welcomes Agreement on Detainee Exchange in Yemen; Affirms GCC Support for a Comprehensive Political Solution Based on the Three References Gulf Cooperation Council 15 May 2026
  • No: 91, 15 May 2026, Regarding the Agreement Between Yemeni Parties on the Release of Detainees Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 May 2026

  • UN Relief Chief urges Security Council to sustain Syria's recovery UNOCHA 15 May 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on the humanitarian situation in Syria by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • From Tragedy to Opportunity, 'New Syria' Is Writing Its Own Story, Security Council Hears, as Speakers Laud Progress, Call for Sustained Recovery UN Security Council 15 May 2026 -- While Syria has seen progress towards accountability and sustained international engagement, the Security Council heard today that unresolved tensions, economic hardship and repeated violations of Syria's sovereignty persist as the country charts its path forward in a volatile region.
  • The UK will continue to work with partners to deliver a more peaceful and prosperous future for the Syrian people: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 15 May 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Syria.

  • Security Council Press Statement on Terrorist Attacks in Mali UN Security Council 15 May 2026 -- The members of the Security Council condemned in the strongest terms the heinous and cowardly terrorist attacks in several locations across Mali on 25 April 2026 and in the days that followed, including 6 May 2026, which resulted in the loss of life and injury.

  • Risk of Famine persists as nearly 19.5 million people face acute food insecurity in Sudan UNICEF 15 May 2026 -- The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), the World Food Programme (WFP) and UNICEF warned today that nearly 19.5 million people - two out of every five people in Sudan - are currently facing crisis levels of acute food insecurity (IPC Phase 3 or above) across Sudan, according to the latest Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) analysis.
  • Risk of Famine Continues to Stalk Families in Sudan as Hunger Deepens WFP 15 May 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by the World Food Programme's (WFP) Director of Emergencies and Response, Ross Smith, to whom quoted text may be attributed - at a press briefing in Geneva today.
  • Joint News Release - WFP/FAO/UNICEF: Risk of Famine persists as nearly 19.5 million people face acute food insecurity in Sudan WFP 15 May 2026 -- The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), the World Food Programme (WFP) and UNICEF warned today that nearly 19.5 million people - two out of every five people in Sudan - are currently facing crisis levels of acute food insecurity (IPC Phase 3 or above) across Sudan, according to the latest Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) analysis.
  • Statement by the Minister of International Development on hunger in Sudan Government of Norway 15 May 2026 -- 'The latest UN figures on food security and hunger in Sudan show that the situation remains extremely grave. It is difficult to comprehend that more than 19 million people are facing acute food insecurity, and that millions remain at emergency or catastrophic levels of hunger', said Minister of International Development Åsmund Aukrust.

  • Somalia at 'real risk of famine' as Middle East war fallout continues UN News 15 May 2026 -- At least six million people in Somalia are going days without enough food, UN aid teams warned on Friday, highlighting that nearly two million of this number are young children "at high risk of illness or death".
  • Joint News Release - OCHA/FAO/WFP/UNICEF: UN agencies warn of worsening hunger and malnutrition crisis in Somalia as famine risk emerges WFP 15 May 2026 -- MOGADISHU, Somalia - The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA), the UN Children's Fund (UNICEF), and the UN World Food Programme (WFP) warned today of a rapidly intensifying hunger emergency in Somalia that is pushing 6 million people - 31 percent of the population - into critical levels of food insecurity (IPC Phase 3 or above) between April and June 2026. This now includes one of the worst malnutrition crises in the world impacting approximately 1.9 million children, of whom 493,000 face severe acute malnutrition, who are 12 times at higher risk of death compared to well-nourished children.

News Reports

  • House Intelligence Committee Holds Briefing with FBI on Recent Terror Attacks As Part of 9/11 Review Effort US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 15 May 2026 -- As part of the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence's bipartisan review of the 9/11 Commission intelligence recommendations announced on September 11th last year, the Committee held a closed briefing with the FBI's Counterterrorism Division on recent terrorist attacks carried out in the United States, including the mass shooting in Austin, Texas, the Gracie Mansion Bomb Plot in New York City, and the Hezbollah-Inspired Attack on Temple Israel in Michigan. The briefers also provided the Members with insight into the trends the FBI is seeing around recent attempts to carry out terror attacks and how the threat landscape, and therefore their response to detect and deter, has evolved since September 11, 2001.

  • Congo reports new Ebola virus disease outbreak Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 May 2026 -- Health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo have confirmed a new Ebola virus disease outbreak, the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) said Friday, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • Iraqi National Arrested and Charged with Providing Material Support to Iranian-Backed Terrorist Organizations and Directing Attacks Targeting U.S. Citizens and Interests US Dept. of Justice 15 May 2026 -- The Justice Department announced today the arrest of Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, an Iraqi national and senior member of Kata'ib Hizballah, a U.S. designated foreign terrorist organization (FTO).
  • Welcoming the Arrest of Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi US Dept. of State 15 May 2026 -- President Trump has pledged that anyone who hurts Americans, or is planning to hurt Americans, will be found and held accountable.

  • NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station NASA 15 May 2026 -- The 34th SpaceX commercial resupply mission under contract with NASA is headed to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments after lifting off at 6:05 p.m. EDT Friday on a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida.

  • Meeting on economic issues President of Russia 15 May 2026 -- The President held a meeting on economic issues.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's participation in BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 May 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's statement and answers to media questions at a news conference following BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting, New Delhi, May 15, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 May 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Foreign Minister of Malaysia Mohamad Hasan, New Delhi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 May 2026
  • Russia, India Set to Boost Supplies of Russian Hydrocarbons - Lavrov Sputnik 15 May 2026 -- Russia and India are determined to increase supplies of Russian hydrocarbons, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Friday.
  • Visit of Foreign Minister of the Russian Federation, H.E. Mr. Sergei Lavrov, to India (May 13-15, 2026) India MEA 15 May 2026 -- The Foreign Minister of the Russian Federation, H.E. Mr. Sergei Lavrov, visited India for the BRICS Foreign Ministers' meeting on 14-15 May 2026.
  • Veteran Russian Dissident Reportedly Left Suicide Note Blaming Putin RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- Nina Litvinova, a prominent Russian human rights activist and dissident whose suicide was reported recently by Russian media, left a note blaming President Vladimir Putin for her death, according to her cousin.
  • Ukrainian Intelligence Increasingly Attempts to Destabilize Russian Society - Russian Foreign Intel Sputnik 15 May 2026 -- Ukrainian special services have recently stepped up their efforts to destabilize Russian society, the Russian Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) said on Friday.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 15, 2026 PRC MofA 15 May 2026
  • Trump, Xi Strike Stabilizing Tone At Beijing Summit Shaped By Iran Conflict RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump departed Beijing on May 15 after a two-day summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping that highlighted how the war in Iran is reshaping the contours of their relationship and testing whether the rivals can coordinate to tackle global crises.
  • Xi, Trump have private meeting in Beijing PRC MND 15 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump had a private meeting on Friday in the Zhongnanhai leadership compound in central Beijing.
  • President Xi Jinping Holds a Private Meeting with U.S. President Donald J. Trump at Zhongnanhai PRC MofA 15 May 2026 -- On the morning of May 15, President Xi Jinping held a private meeting with U.S. President Donald J. Trump at Zhongnanhai.
  • Senate Foreign Relations Democrats Statement on Trump-Xi Summit US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 15 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chris Coons (D-DE), Chris Murphy (D-CT), Tim Kaine (D-VA), Jeff Merkley (D-OR), Cory Booker (D-NJ), Brian Schatz (D-HI), Chris Van Hollen (D-MD), Tammy Duckworth (D-IL) and Jacky Rosen (D-NV) released the following statement after the conclusion of President Trump's summit with Chinese President Xi in Beijing.
  • Trump, Xi Open Second Day Of Talks With Iran Crisis Hanging Over Summit RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping opened the final day of their Beijing summit with the war in Iran increasingly taking center stage over the trade disputes and technology tensions that had originally been expected to dominate the meeting between the world's two largest powers.
  • Xi, Trump have restricted meeting in Beijing Global Times 15 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump had a restricted meeting on Friday in Beijing.
  • Chinese FM briefs on US President Donald Trump's visit to China: the two presidents hold in-depth exchange of views on major issues concerning the two countries and the world, reach a series of new common understandings Global Times 15 May 2026 -- Q: President Xi Jinping yesterday held state events for the visiting U.S. President Donald Trump, which drew close attention from home and abroad. Can you give us more details?
  • Chinese, U.S. presidents agree on new vision for bilateral ties in Beijing talks People's Daily 15 May 2026 -- In a meeting closely watched by the world, Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday agreed on a new vision of building a constructive bilateral relationship of strategic stability.
  • Xi, Trump reach series of new common understandings: China's foreign ministry People's Daily 15 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump had an in-depth exchange of views on major issues concerning the two countries and the world and reached a series of new common understandings, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said Friday.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on U.S. President Donald Trump's Visit to China PRC MofA 15 May 2026
  • China stands ready to maintain close exchanges with UK, Chinese FM says on British foreign secretary's reported China visit Global Times 15 May 2026 -- When responding to a foreign media reporter's question citing a Reuters report on Friday that British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will visit China according to sources, and asking whether China could confirm the visit and what expectations China has for Cooper's planned visit, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday that both China and the UK are permanent members of the UN Security Council and major economies in the world. To develop a sustained and steady China-UK comprehensive strategic partnership is an important common understanding reached between the leaders of the two countries.
  • China successfully launches first special satellite for energy engineering Global Times 15 May 2026 -- China on Friday successfully sent PowerChina-1, the country's first dedicated satellite for energy engineering, into its predetermined orbit with a Lijian-1 Y13 carrier rocket at the Dongfeng commercial space innovation pilot zone in northwest China, the Global Times learned from the developer on Friday.

  • Taiwan should closely watch U.S. policy despite no stated changes: Experts CNA 15 May 2026 -- Taiwan should remain alert to possible subtle shifts in Washington's approach toward Taipei, despite the absence of stated changes in the United States' Taiwan policy following the summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平), an expert said Friday.
  • White House omission suggests Taiwan non-negotiable: Analysts CNA 15 May 2026 -- The White House's decision not to mention Taiwan in its official summary of talks between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平) suggests Washington does not see the issue as up for negotiation, analysts said Thursday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 15 May 2026

  • S. Korea's special counsel seeks 7.5-year sentence for ex-President Yoon's wife for corruptions People's Daily 15 May 2026 -- South Korea's special counsel on Friday sought a 7.5-year prison sentence for Kim Keon-hee, wife of former President Yoon Suk-yeol, for corruptions including the sale of government posts.

  • Three Iranian satellites in final testing phase: Space Agency Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Iran's space agency has announced that three domestically developed satellites have entered final testing, signaling a acceleration in the country's civilian space program despite Western pressures.

  • Statement by the spokesperson on the formation of a new government in Iraq European External Action Service (EEAS) 15 May 2026 -- The High Representative Kaja Kallas congratulated Iraq on the formation of a new government and wishes to Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi and his government full success in delivering on the aspirations of the Iraqi people. We also extend our congratulations to Fuad Hussein for his re-appointment as Minister of Foreign Affairs and look forward to continue engaging with him.
  • Formation of a New Government in Iraq (Statement by Press Secretary KITAMURA Toshihiro) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 15 May 2026
  • Iranian president vows to continue to support Iraq as new PM sworn in Press TV 15 May 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has vowed that the Islamic Republic will continue to support Iraq and its new government.

  • PM Netanyahu's remarks at the Festive Event at Mercaz HaRav Yeshiva, Marking 59 Years since the Unification of Jerusalem Israel - Prime Minister 15 May 2026

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Foreign Minister of Cuba Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 May 2026
  • Blackouts and shortages disrupt healthcare across Cuba UN News 15 May 2026 -- Hospitals across Cuba are suspending surgeries, struggling to keep lifesaving equipment running and facing severe medicine shortages as blackouts and fuel shortages push the country's healthcare system deeper into crisis, senior UN officials warned on Friday.

  • Visit of Foreign Minister of Chile, H.E. Mr. Francisco Perez Mackenna, to India (May 11-14, 2026) India MEA 15 May 2026 -- H.E. Mr. Francisco Pérez Mackenna, paid an official visit India from 11-14 May 2026. He was accompanied by H.E. Paula Estévez Weinsteina, Vice Minister of International Economic Relations, and representatives of prominent industries in Chile.
  • Prime Minister's visit to the United Arab Emirates (May 15, 2026) India MEA 15 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi paid an official visit to the United Arab Emirates today. He was received at the airport by His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the UAE and accorded a ceremonial welcome.
  • Special Briefing by MEA on BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting (May 15, 2026) India MEA 15 May 2026
  • Transcript of remarks by Foreign Secretary on Prime Minister's visit to the UAE India MEA 15 May 2026

  • The Daily Press Briefing (05/15/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 15 May 2026

  • Minister Anand meets with Minister of State International Affairs of Qatar Canada 15 May 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, met with His Excellency Dr. Mohammed bin Abdulaziz Al-Khulaifi, Minister of State at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the State of Qatar (Qatar).
  • Minister Anand meets with Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of Qatar Canada 15 May 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, met with His Excellency Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs, State of Qatar (Qatar).

  • Japan-U.S. Summit Telephone Talk Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 15 May 2026

  • UAE's West-East 1 Pipeline to double export capacity via Fujairah, bypassing Hormuz Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 May 2026 -- The UAE's Abu Dhabi National Oil Company new West-East 1 Pipeline project is expected to double the company's export capacity through the Emirate of Fujairah, bypassing the Strait of Hormuz, Anadolu Agency reported citing the Abu Dhabi Media Office.

  • Baiba Braže: Jordan is a key partner in Middle East security Latvia MFA 15 May 2026 -- On 15 May 2026, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Baiba Braže, met with the Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, Ayman Safadi, highlighting Jordan's significant contribution to stabilising the situation in the Middle East and its support in addressing the refugee crisis in the region. This was the first bilateral visit by a Jordanian Foreign Minister to Latvia in the history of relations between the two countries.

  • 'Seismic shift' toward Aussie orbit likely under Solomon Islands' new prime minister Radio Free Asia 15 May 2026 -- The Solomon Islands' choice of longtime opposition leader Matthew Wale as its new prime minister could be a sign that a diplomatic shift toward Canberra is likely, while the Pacific island nation still engages moderately with Beijing, experts told Radio Free Asia.

  • Freedom For Foreigners, Limitations For Locals As Turkmenistan Opens To Tourism RFE/RL 15 May 2026 -- Soon before she arrived in Turkmenistan in early 2026, Canadian traveler Elyse Williamsreceived a warning from her tour company not to venture far from her hotel in the authoritarian country and to avoid taking photos while alone on the streets.

  • World News in Brief: UN relief vehicle struck in Ukraine, emergency airdrops in South Sudan, backlash against LGBTIQ+ rights UN News 15 May 2026 -- The Secretary-General is alarmed that a clearly marked United Nations vehicle was struck twice in Kherson city in Ukraine on 14 May, his spokesperson said in a statement.
